comment: Wall-clock UX budgets for the CI-only interaction-latency lane (e2e/interaction-latency.spec.ts, a Playwright project that exists only under HARNESS_PERF_LANE=1 — never in the validate chain or the Stop hook). Budgets are DELIBERATELY generous: shared CI runners are noisy, so this lane catches step-function regressions (a 300ms main-thread stall on keydown), not 10% drifts — the in-chain perf-budget gate stays the tight relative canary. Human-tuned data (write-guard-protected, seeded — update never rewrites it): re-baseline only in a reviewed commit, from a local `HARNESS_PERF_LANE=1 pnpm exec playwright test --project perf` median with ~6-8x headroom. See docs/harness/gates-catalog.md (CI-only lanes).
memoryComment: The memory ceiling (e2e/memory.spec.ts): mount and unmount every screen `loops` times via the app's own in-page router, force a GC, and compare against the steady state measured after warmup. listenerGrowth counts LIVE listeners on window+document — the targets that outlive every component, where a missing removeEventListener is a true leak that nothing will ever collect. It is an integer and it is sharp: measured on this scaffold, a correct tree grows by 0 and a single un-removed listener per mount grows by 16 over 8 cycles. The allowance of 2 is for a lazily-registered singleton, not for leaking; the honest target is 0. heapGrowthKb is a deliberately COARSE backstop for retention that holds no listener at all (an ever-growing cache): the dev server itself drifts by hundreds of kB per run, so it will catch megabyte-scale retention and nothing subtler — it does not pretend otherwise. CDP's Nodes and JSEventListeners counters are deliberately NOT asserted: calibrated against a real injected leak they did not move (or moved identically on the clean app), and a check that cannot tell the defect from its absence is a fake control. The file is write-guard-protected — raising these is a reviewed human decision.
